nativeRange.habitat: Carbonate sand patches and rubble caves on clear-water coral reefs, typically at depths of 25–50 m

decorPreferences: fine sand substrate (5+ cm deep for burrowing)
rubble or rock for cave formation

diet.notes: Has been documented feeding on small crustaceans, zooplankton, and meaty invertebrates near its burrow. In captivity tends to accept frozen mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, and finely chopped meaty foods; may require some coaxing initially. Target feeding near the burrow entrance is generally advised.

summary: The Orange Stripe Prawn Goby (Amblyeleotris randalli) is a Western Pacific reef fish known for its symbiotic partnership with pistol shrimp, with which it shares a burrow. It is generally considered beginner-friendly and reef-safe, and does best when kept with a compatible pistol shrimp in a sand-substrate system with a well-fitted lid.
careNotes:
The Orange Stripe Prawn Goby is best known for its symbiosis with Alpheid pistol shrimp — most often Alpheus ochrostriatus or a closely related species. In this partnership, the shrimp constructs and maintains a shared burrow while the goby stands watch at the entrance for approaching predators. Providing a compatible pistol shrimp in the aquarium is strongly encouraged, as the partnership appears to benefit both animals and supports natural behavior.

At least 5 cm of fine sand substrate is generally recommended to allow burrowing. The species tends to occupy the bottom zone, spending most of its time hovering just above the sand near the burrow entrance, and open sand patches for foraging should be included in the aquascape.

In the wild this species has been collected from depths of 25–50 m on clear-water reefs, so it is not typically a shallow-water, high-light fish. Moderate lighting is generally sufficient, and stable, mature reef-system water parameters — consistent temperature, salinity, and water quality — are generally advised.

Escape risk is considered high: like most shrimp gobies, this species has been reported to jump from open aquaria, so a well-fitted lid is essential. The goby is also known to dart rapidly when startled or stressed.

Conspecific aggression tends to be moderate; two males housed together in a small tank may compete for territory. A mated male–female pair tends to coexist more readily.

Feeding in captivity tends to be moderately straightforward — most specimens will accept frozen mysis shrimp and Artemia once acclimated. Newly acquired individuals may initially be reluctant to feed away from the burrow, so target feeding near the burrow entrance is generally advised until the fish settles in.

primary.name: FishBase
primary.notes: Used as effective primary source. LiveAquaria (designated primary) was blocked — all attempts returned the homepage rather than any species product page. FishBase provided maximum size, temperature range, depth, native range, taxonomy, habitat, and trophic level.

additional.name: LiveAquaria
additional.notes: Designated primary source per manifest. Multiple URL patterns attempted (category browse, search, direct pcatid guesses) — all returned the LiveAquaria homepage without reaching a species product page. Swapped to FishBase as effective primary per playbook fallback rule.

additional.name: Wikipedia
additional.notes: Cross-verification source. Confirmed species identity, symbiotic relationship with Alpheus ochrostriatus, carnivorous diet, Western Pacific range. Reported size as ~9 cm vs FishBase 12 cm SL — see careNotes. Used for taxonomy cross-check and biology confirmation.

additional.name: FishBase (Country List)
additional.notes: Confirmed native range across 13 territories in Western Pacific including Indonesia, Japan, Philippines, Australia, Palau, Solomon Islands, Fiji, Papua New Guinea, Malaysia, New Caledonia, Micronesia.

additional.name: Blue Zoo Aquatics
additional.notes: Listed as "Randall's Goby" (product ID 546). Provided trade-level husbandry data: min tank 24 gallons, care level beginner, temperament semi-aggressive, reef compatible, adult size ~5 inches, diet carnivore accepting frozen mysis and prepared meaty foods. Also sold as a pistol-shrimp combo pair (product ID 19888). Used to resolve aquarium-source gap flagged in needs_review.
